mock1.title: A2 Key — Reading and Writing (MOCK 1)
mock1.note: Parts 1–5 are auto-scored. Parts 6–7 (Writing) are practice tasks and not auto-scored.

parts.instructions: For each question, look at the text and choose the correct answer A, B or C.

questions.text: CYCLISTS MUST GET OFF AND PUSH THEIR BIKES ON THE WOODEN BRIDGE. THE BRIDGE IS SHARED WITH WALKERS.
questions.stem: What does the notice ask cyclists to do?
options: Walk with their bikes across the bridge.

questions.text: Hi Grandma, I can't come to your house at three as planned — my bus is running late. I should arrive by four instead. Please don't start cooking until I text you. See you soon!
questions.stem: Why has Leon sent this message?
options: To say he will not visit today.
To tell his grandma he will be late.
To ask his grandma to cook earlier.

questions.subject: Gallery trip — bring a packed lunch
questions.body: Dear students, the gallery café will be closed for repairs on the day of our trip, so please bring your own packed lunch. The bus still leaves at 8.30, and we will return by 4 p.m. as usual.
questions.stem: What does the teacher want students to remember?
options: The trip has been moved to a different day.
They must buy lunch at the gallery café.
They need to bring food with them.

questions.text: Sam — the parcel you ordered arrived while you were out. I've left it with our neighbour Mr Price at number 14 because it was too big for the letterbox. — Dad
questions.stem: Where can Sam find his parcel?

questions.text: PAY BEFORE YOU RETURN TO YOUR CAR. KEEP YOUR TICKET ON THE DASHBOARD. CARS WITHOUT A TICKET MAY BE FINED.
questions.stem: What must drivers do?
options: Pay when they arrive at the car park.
Pay before going back to their car.
Show their ticket at the exit gate.

questions.text: GLASS AND PAPER ONLY IN THESE BINS. PLEASE TAKE GARDEN WASTE TO THE GREEN AREA AT THE BACK.
questions.stem: What does this notice tell visitors?
options: All types of rubbish go in these bins.
Garden waste must be left somewhere else.
Glass is not allowed at this centre.

parts.instructions: Read about three young people and their weekend jobs. For each question, choose the correct person (A, B or C). The people may be chosen more than once.

sources.label: A
sources.name: Diego
sources.text: On Saturdays I help my uncle at his fruit and vegetable stall in the market. I start very early, at six o'clock, which is hard, but I enjoy chatting to the customers. My uncle pays me, and I'm saving the money for a new bike. I don't really like the cold mornings in winter, but the rest of the year it's great.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Yara
sources.text: I walk dogs for three families near my house on Sunday afternoons. I love animals, so it doesn't really feel like work. The owners pay me, but honestly I would do it for free. The only difficult part is when it rains and the dogs get muddy! I'm hoping to study to be a vet one day.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Otto
sources.text: I work in a small bakery on Saturday mornings. I don't get paid much, but I get free bread and cakes to take home, which my family loves! I have to be there at seven, so I don't enjoy getting up early. One day I'd like to open my own café, so this is good practice for me.

questions.stem: Which person is saving up to buy something?
Which person would happily do their job without being paid?
Which person receives food instead of much money?
Which two people dislike starting work very early?
Which person hopes their job will help with a future business?

parts.instructions: Read the text about a girl who built a weather station. Choose the correct answer A, B or C.
parts.passage:
Maya is thirteen and lives in a small town where the weather changes very quickly. Last winter, she became interested in why the local forecasts were so often wrong for her area. Her science teacher suggested she build her own simple weather station to record the conditions in her own garden.

Maya used some old equipment her grandfather had kept in his shed, including a rain gauge and a thermometer. She also bought a cheap sensor online to measure the wind. At first, she found it difficult to connect the sensor to her computer, and she nearly gave up. Her older sister, who studies computing at college, helped her fix the problem one weekend.

For three months, Maya wrote down the temperature, rainfall and wind speed every single morning before school. She was surprised to discover that her garden was usually two degrees colder than the official figures for the nearest city. She thinks this is because her town sits in a valley.

Maya now shares her measurements with a few neighbours who garden, so they know when to protect their plants from frost. Her teacher has asked her to present her project to the whole class next month. Maya says she would like to study to become a real weather scientist.

questions.stem: Why did Maya start her project?
options: Her teacher gave the whole class the task.
She wondered why local forecasts were often wrong.

questions.stem: What problem did Maya have at first?
options: She could not afford any equipment.
She could not connect the sensor to her computer.
She could not find anywhere to put the station.

questions.stem: What surprised Maya about her measurements?
options: Her garden was colder than the city figures.
It rained more than the forecasts said.
The wind was much stronger than expected.

questions.stem: How do Maya's neighbours use her information?
options: To decide when to water their plants.
To know when frost might harm their plants.
To plan when to cut the grass.

questions.stem: What does Maya hope to do in the future?

parts.instructions: For each gap (19–24), choose the best word A, B, C or D.
parts.title: The Story of Chocolate
parts.body: Chocolate comes from the seeds of the cacao tree, which (19)____ in hot, wet countries near the equator. People in Central America were the first to (20)____ it, more than three thousand years ago. At that time, chocolate was not sweet at all; instead, it was a bitter drink. It only (21)____ popular in Europe much later, after explorers brought the seeds back across the sea. For a long time, chocolate was so expensive that (22)____ rich people could afford it. Today, factories produce huge (23)____ of chocolate every year, and it is enjoyed almost (24)____ in the world.

parts.instructions: For each gap (25–30), write ONE word.
parts.title: Email to a new pen friend
parts.body:
Dear Hana,

Thank you (25)____ your letter — it was lovely to hear from you! My name is Kofi and I am writing to you (26)____ Ghana. I am fourteen years old and I have two younger brothers.

In my free time, I (27)____ playing the drums in a band with my friends. We practise every Friday. (28)____ the weekend, I usually visit my grandmother, who lives near the beach.

Please tell me more (29)____ your country and your hobbies. I hope we (30)____ meet one day!

Best wishes,
Kofi

prompts.instructions:
Your English friend Robin is coming to visit your town next month. Write an email to Robin. In your email: suggest one place to visit together / tell Robin what clothes to bring / ask one question about the visit.
Write 35–45 words.

Write a story for your English class. Your story must begin with this sentence:
The lights suddenly went out, and the whole house was dark.
Write 35–45 words.

mock2.title: A2 Key — Reading and Writing (MOCK 2)
mock2.note: Parts 1–5 are auto-scored. Parts 6–7 (Writing) are practice tasks and not auto-scored.

parts.instructions: For each question, look at the text and choose the correct answer A, B or C.

questions.text: THE CAFÉ IS NOW OPEN ON THE FIRST FLOOR. ENTRY TO THE MUSEUM IS FREE, BUT THE SPECIAL DINOSAUR EXHIBITION COSTS £5.
questions.stem: What does the notice tell visitors?
options: Everyone must pay to enter the museum.
There is a charge for one special exhibition.
The café is closed at the moment.

questions.header: From: Coach Bianca → To: Football team
questions.text: Team — tomorrow's match is still on, but the pitch is wet, so wear your boots with longer studs. Meet at the changing rooms at 9, not 9.30. Bring a warm jacket for after the game.
questions.stem: What is Coach Bianca telling the team?
options: They should arrive earlier than usual.
They need to bring their own footballs.

questions.subject: App update needed
questions.body: Dear member, to keep renting our bikes, please update the City Bikes app before Friday. The old version will stop working this weekend. You will not be able to unlock a bike without the new version. Updating is free.
questions.stem: Why has City Bikes sent this email?
options: To tell members the price has gone up.
To remind members to update their app.
To say the bikes will no longer be available.

questions.text: Marco — I've gone to collect your sister from her dance class. Your dinner is in the oven, but please don't turn the oven off — I'm cooking something for tomorrow at the same time. — Mum
questions.stem: What does Mum want Marco to do?

questions.text: DO NOT FEED THE BIRDS. BREAD MAKES THEM ILL. YOU MAY TAKE PHOTOGRAPHS, BUT PLEASE STAY BEHIND THE FENCE.
questions.stem: What does the sign ask visitors not to do?

questions.text: FROM SEPTEMBER, YOU CAN BORROW UP TO FOUR BOOKS AT ONCE, NOT TWO. PLEASE RETURN THEM WITHIN THREE WEEKS.
questions.stem: What is new at the library?
options: Students can keep books for longer.
Students can take out more books than before.
Students must pay to borrow books.

parts.instructions: Read about three friends who entered a photography competition. For each question, choose the correct person (A, B or C). The people may be chosen more than once.

sources.label: A
sources.name: Noor
sources.text: I took my photo at the beach very early in the morning, when there was almost nobody there. It shows the sun coming up over the water. I used my mum's old camera, not my phone, because the quality is better. I didn't win, but the judges said my photo was the most peaceful, and that made me really happy.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Hugo
sources.text: My photo was of my little brother laughing at the dinner table. I just used my phone — I think the moment matters more than the equipment. I actually won second prize, which surprised me! The prize was a voucher for a camera shop, so now I want to buy a proper camera and get better.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Elena
sources.text: I took hundreds of photos of birds in my garden over the summer before I chose one to enter. It took a lot of patience because birds move so fast! I used my phone, like Hugo. I didn't get a prize this time, but I've decided to enter again next year with a photo of the city at night.

questions.stem: Which person won a prize?
Which person used a camera rather than a phone?
Which person waited a long time to get the right picture?
Which two people took their photo with a phone?
Which person already has a plan for next year's competition?

parts.instructions: Read the text about a mountain rescue dog. Choose the correct answer A, B or C.
parts.passage:
Bruno is a large brown dog who works with a mountain rescue team in the north of Italy. His owner, Paolo, is a rescue worker who has trained dogs for over ten years. Bruno is special because he can find people who are lost in the snow, even when they are buried deep below it.

Bruno was not always good at his job. As a young dog, he was easily frightened by the noise of the rescue helicopter, and Paolo worried he would never be ready. Paolo spent many months slowly teaching Bruno to stay calm. He gave Bruno a small reward every time the dog was brave.

Last winter, two walkers got lost during a heavy snowstorm. The rescue team could not see anything, and the weather was too bad for the helicopter to fly. Bruno led the team for almost two hours through the snow until he found the walkers, who were cold but safe. Without Bruno, Paolo says, they might not have found them in time.

Bruno is now quite famous in his town, and children often ask to take photos with him. Paolo says the most important thing is not fame, but the lives that Bruno helps to save. Bruno will keep working for a few more years before he retires to live quietly at Paolo's home.

questions.stem: What is special about Bruno?
options: He can fly in the rescue helicopter.
He can find people buried in the snow.
He is the largest dog on the team.

questions.stem: What problem did Bruno have when he was young?
options: He was afraid of the helicopter's noise.
He did not like the cold.
He could not run fast enough.

questions.stem: How did Paolo help Bruno improve?
options: He took Bruno to a special school.
He rewarded Bruno for being brave.
He let other dogs train with Bruno.

questions.stem: Why was the rescue last winter difficult?
options: The walkers were a long way away.
The helicopter could not fly in the bad weather.

questions.stem: What does Paolo think is most important?
options: That Bruno is famous in the town.
That Bruno helps to save lives.

parts.instructions: For each gap (19–24), choose the best word A, B, C or D.
parts.title: The History of Tea
parts.body: Tea is one of the most popular drinks in the world, but few people know how it was first (19)____ . According to an old story, a Chinese emperor was boiling water when some leaves from a nearby bush (20)____ into his pot by accident. He decided to taste the drink and (21)____ that he liked it very much. For hundreds of years, tea remained a drink that (22)____ people in Asia knew about. Later, traders carried it to Europe, where it quickly became (23)____ . Today, people grow tea in many warm countries, and millions of cups are (24)____ every single day.

parts.instructions: For each gap (25–30), write ONE word.
parts.title: A message about a lost phone
parts.body:
Hi Daniel,

I think I left my phone (25)____ your car yesterday after the cinema. Could you have a look (26)____ me, please? It's a black phone with a blue case.

If you find (27)____ , please don't worry about bringing it round — I can come and collect it (28)____ myself. I'm free (29)____ Saturday afternoon if that suits you.

Thanks so much for your help. I really hope it (30)____ in your car and not somewhere in town!

Speak soon,
Priya

prompts.instructions:
Your English friend Sam wants to start learning a musical instrument. Write an email to Sam. In your email: say which instrument Sam should learn / explain why / ask one question about Sam's plans.
Write 35–45 words.

Write a story for your English class. Your story must begin with this sentence:
It was the strangest thing I had ever seen in the sky.
Write 35–45 words.
